qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Incomplete DoQ frames exhaust the global QUIC worker pool
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a DoQ stream-lifecycle availability bug in core/dnsserver/server_quic.go, while the advisory is a TSIG authentication bypass caused by transport TSIG status handling and missing TSIG verification state on encrypted transports.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's TSIG verification bypass root cause. The session's only final qualifying finding is a separate DoQ worker-pool exhaustion issue rather than the advisory-described transport TSIG authentication bypass.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes a TSIG authentication bypass because plugin/tsig/tsig.go trusts transport writer TsigStatus(), with affected transport-specific behavior called out in core/dnsserver/https.go, core/dnsserver/server_tls.go, core/dnsserver/quic.go, and core/dnsserver/server_grpc.go.
The authoritative session manifest at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_cff35b55/.judge/input_manifest.json lists only one final qualifying finding: doq_incomplete_frame_worker_exhaustion.

That finding's vulnerability_report.json attributes the bug to early acquisition of the shared QUIC worker pool and blocking partial-frame reads in core/dnsserver/server_quic.go, which is an availability failure and not the advisory's missing TSIG verification/authentication logic.
